svg.title: Generic W8A16 FFN schedule, SmolLM2-135M instance
svg.desc: Cycle-accurate FTLPU schedule for gate and up projection, SwiGLU, cross-hemisphere copy, and down projection.
svg.text: Generic W8A16 FFN Pipeline: SmolLM2-135M Instance
X[32,576] fp16, gate/up/down int8, hidden=1536, output[32,576] fp16; generated by FTLPU Stream -> Schedule lowering

g.text: 24 output pairs x 18 K blocks; issue interval 38 cycles
38-cycle K blocks; pair boundary derived from MEM conflicts

svg.text: 2. Current compiler schedule: conservative SwiGLU tail
Measured Schedule IR, one hemisphere shown. All 18 projection partials, including P17, accumulate to SRAM. SwiGLU starts only after all projection pairs complete.

g.text: KB0..KB17: 18 K=32 blocks, each begins 38 cycles after the previous block.
No SwiGLU is issued in this local 0..750 projection trace.
After projection pair 23: accumulator read -> first Swish at global cycle 16,469 -> hidden write.
This local 0..750 view shows the first N-pair only. P17 writes local accumulator SRAM; no fixed SwiGLU overlap batch is emitted.
The global timeline above is authoritative for the tail: all 24 projection pairs finish first, then local SRAM is read through W0..W7 and SwiGLU begins at 16,469.

svg.text: One hidden row / hemisphere event. VXM computes (gate * sigmoid(gate)) * up without materializing a sigmoid tensor.

g.text: VXM pass-through keeps gate * up aligned
write local slices 21/22/23/29 at t+11 / t+13
sigmoid(gate) = 1 / (1 + exp(-gate)); final value = sigmoid(gate) * (gate * up) = SiLU(gate) * up.

svg.text: The first 47 reductions retain FP32 in MEM; reduction 47 changes ACC destination to stream and casts the completed result to FP16.

g.text: All intervals come from target constraints: MEM slice conflicts, transport latency, 16-stream IW load, MXM pipeline skew, accumulators, and cross-hemisphere copy.
